mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A month-over-month decrease in FEDFUNDS signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, supporting corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. Month-over-month employment growth produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns fail to materialize in > 50% of months with positive PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below a critical threshold signals weakening demand-side momentum and forward recession risk. When HOUST falls below 1200 thousand units, equity investors reprice downward risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where HOUST < 1200, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above trend, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. When INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, equity markets reprice earnings upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact such that rising inflation combined with elevated rates signals policy tightening that compresses equity multiples. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, S&P 500 returns turn negative over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 252.733 and FEDFUNDS at 2.27% as of 2019-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and employment growth (PAYEMS) interact such that rising unemployment despite positive payroll growth signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk. When UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month while PAYEMS growth slows below 100k, S&P 500 returns turn negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% and PAYEMS at 150263.0 thousand as of 2019-01-15
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand momentum. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously, forward consumer spending deteriorates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513531.0 million and HOUST at 1256.0 thousand as of 2019-01-15
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline signals supply-side weakness and forward earnings compression. When INDPRO falls below 109.0 (year-over-year contraction), equity markets reprice downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.
